text:
👋 **This is a pre-loaded example — ready for you to run.** I've set up a Nanopore 10x 3′ single-cell experiment: the reads are already uploaded (see **reads.fastq.gz** in the Files panel) and I've built the expected spec from the description below.

**Your turn:** open **Extracted spec** in the viewer to review it, then click **Confirm spec** — QC runs automatically. It will flag *TSO concatemers* (fused reads); the **Computational fixes** panel can then split them and re-score.

Oxford Nanopore long-read sequencing of 10x Chromium Single Cell 3′ cDNA (v3/v3.1). Not paired short-read — every read is one full-length molecule, one FASTQ of long reads. Molecule (5′→3'): R1 handle (partial TruSeq Read 1, CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT) → 16-nt cell barcode → 12-nt UMI → poly(T) + VN anchor → cDNA insert → TSO at the 3′ end as its reverse complement. Reads occur in both orientations. Known problem: template-switch concatemers — some reads carry an internal TSO/adapter2 copy (two cDNAs fused), which we'd split downstream. Direct-ligation ONT prep.

I extracted the expected read/library structure — open **Extracted spec** in the Files panel to review it. The gist:

- **Platform:** Oxford Nanopore (long-read); single full-length read per molecule (no R1/R2 pairing)
- **Read (5′→3'):** R1 handle → `cell_barcode:16` → `umi:12` → poly(T) → cDNA insert → TSO (reverse-complement) at the 3′ end
- **Watch for:** internal TSO/adapter2 copies = template-switch concatemers / fused reads

Review it in the viewer, then click **Confirm spec** to run QC on the uploaded reads.
